name: Brain-derived neurotrophic factor
summary: BDNF encodes brain-derived neurotrophic factor, a neurotrophin that supports neuronal survival, synaptic plasticity, and neurogenesis. The Val66Met variant (rs6265) affects activity-dependent secretion and is associated with depression, anxiety, and stress-related phenotypes. BDNF is implicated in the pathophysiology of major depressive disorder and schizophrenia; stress and early adversity interact with genotype. Expression is regulated by exercise, antidepressants, and environmental enrichment.

regulatory_notes.promoter_activity: Activity-dependent transcription; CREB and calcium signaling.
regulatory_notes.enhancer_associations: Val66Met affects BDNF trafficking and secretion.

mechanistic_hypotheses.hypothesis: Val66Met carriers show heightened stress sensitivity and reduced hippocampal volume; childhood adversity amplifies depression risk.

references.title: GTEx Consortium atlas of genetic regulatory effects
The BDNF Val66Met polymorphism and stress
